LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APRIL-18-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SALEM MANSOIR'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S "MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR THE MOVING PAPERS' NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1113 AND 3.1324. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-05-2024 Continued To Sep-25-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-14-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: PLAINTIFF SALEM MANSOIR'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file the second amended complaint is granted.

The court liberally allows amendments to pleadings. (Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) The court, however, may deny leave to amend where a proposed amendment is untimely as a matter of law. (Yee v. Mobilehome Park Rental Board (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 1409, 1429.) Denial of leave to amend is proper where the complaint "shows on its face that it is incapable of amendment." (Eghtesad v. State Farm Gen. Ins. Co. (2020) 51 Cal.App.5th 406, 411.)

In this case, the proposed second amended complaint alleges that defendant assured plaintiff that insurance would cover his deep root cleaning of September 12, 2018. Plaintiff further alleges: "Defendants did not inform Plaintiff that the insurance claim was not submitted in the time period Defendants had represented to Plaintiff. Plaintiff did not learn that Defendants' above mentioned representations were in fact misrepresentations until February 2, 2021, when he received his first bill for the full amount of the deep root cleaning." (Friedman Decl., Ex. A [Proposed Second Amended Complaint, pars. 13-14.]

"A plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his or her claim would be barred by the applicable orthodox statute of limitations, and who intends to rely on the discovery rule to toll the orthodox limitation period, 'must specifically plead facts which show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. [Citations.]" (Camsi IV v. Hunter Technology Corp. (1991) 230 Cal.App.3d 1525, 1536-1537.) Plaintiff's allegations are sufficient to invoke the discovery rule and the proposed fraud claim is not facially time-barred. Plaintiff filed this action on July 28, 2023, and his discovery of the harm in 2021 falls with the three-year statute of limitations for fraud. (CCP 338(d).) The motion is therefore granted.

The Court to review the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Jaime. Matter not reported.
(302/RCE)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-18-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF SALEM MANSOIR's Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion for leave to amend" is denied without prejudice for the moving papers' noncompliance with California Rules of Court 3.1113 and 3.1324.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.
